document.name: Staten Island Children's Museum
document.shortDescription: Hands-on children’s museum on the Snug Harbor campus (Wed–Sun) with exhibits, workshops, and family programs; advance tickets recommended.
document.longDescription:
Staten Island Children’s Museum invites families to explore interactive exhibits, create art in workshops, and catch performances; the official visit page highlights spaces such as Pets & People, Sea of Boats, House About It, Big Games, Great Explorations, and Portia’s Playhouse, and recommends buying tickets in advance and checking the calendar for workshops and events.

Daily public hours on the visit page: Wednesday through Sunday 10:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Members-only hour (photo ID required): Wednesday through Friday 9:00 a.m. to 10:00 a.m. The museum lists closures on New Year’s Day, Easter Day, Memorial Day, Independence Day, Labor Day, Thanksgiving Day, and Christmas Day.

Admission from the same page: adults $10; children $10; children under one year free; members free.

Location and phone from the directions page: 1000 Richmond Terrace, Staten Island, NY 10301, on the grounds of Snug Harbor Cultural Center & Botanical Garden; phone (718) 273-2060. Public transportation guidance on that page: Staten Island Ferry, then S40 bus from St. George Ferry Terminal to the Snug Harbor Road stop (about 5–10 minutes).

Sources: https://www.sichildrensmuseum.org/visit https://sichildrensmuseum.org/directions/
document.announcementDescription: Wednesday–Friday 9–10 a.m. members-only hour (photo ID required) per official visit page.
